headline: Down Almost 9% in 1 Week, Is This Your Chance to Buy Starbucks Stock?
summary: Starbucks is finally seeing meaningful customer traffic growth at its stores. But the coffee chain's turnaround is coming at a steep cost to its profitability.

headline: Dimming Hopes for Rate Cuts Drag Down U.S. Stocks
summary: Stocks fell on Wednesday, after one wholesale inflation gauge came in hotter than expected and Federal Reserve Chair  Jerome Powell  highlighted the risks that higher energy prices will lift inflation.  With the Iran war sending energy prices sharply higher and threatening to stoke price pressures, analysts said the question is no longer when officials will cut rates, but whether they can credibly keep telling markets to expect a rate cut at all.  “Their hands are tied,” said  Michael O’Rourke,  chief market strategist at JonesTrading.

headline: Coty Revamps Board With Appointment of Five New Independent Directors
summary: NEW YORK, March 18, 2026--Regulatory News: Coty Inc. (NYSE: COTY) (Paris: COTY) ("Coty" or "the Company") today announced that its Board of Directors (the "Board") has appointed five new independent directors as part of a comprehensive Board refreshment. The new directors are Carsten Fischer, Alia Gogi, Robert Kunze-Concewitz, Maria Carla Liuni, and Stephanie Plaines. headline: Why Western playbooks fail in China — and what it takes for brands to compete
summary: China’s $7 trillion consumer market was once a reliable growth engine for Western brands, but as competition intensifies and trends move at digital speed, success if no longer a given. Still, they haven’t given up. Global companies are now turning to private equity partners to navigate a market that is less forgiving than before, but will that be enough to stay competitive?

headline: Starbucks Stock Gets Downgraded. Why the Good Times Can’t Continue.
summary: Starbucks stock got a downgrade from RBC Capital analysts, citing “ongoing labor” and other future investments the coffee chain may need to make.  RBC downgraded  Starbucks  to Sector Perform from Outperform in a note published late Tuesday, while maintaining its price target of $105.  Shares fell 0.9% to $96.70 in premarket trading Wednesday.

headline: Shareholder Push Puts Starbucks Board Oversight And Valuation Under Scrutiny
summary: Shareholders are seeking to remove Starbucks (NasdaqGS:SBUX) board members Jørgen Vig Knudstorp and Beth Ford over concerns about stalled union negotiations and labor oversight. The campaign follows warnings that Starbucks may be underestimating financial and reputational risks linked to ongoing labor disputes. More than 680 Starbucks stores have voted to unionize since 2021, highlighting a continuing labor and governance challenge for the company. Starbucks, best known for its global...
